target: 1_长程对话中的交互式记忆评测_Jiayang Cheng_20260702_234408\main.tex

findings.issue: The document states controlling claims early at the document, chapter, and subsection levels, often using explicit conclusion-first boxes or opening paragraphs.
findings.recommendation: Maintain this pattern through final compilation; it is the strongest structural feature of the artifact.

findings.location: Section 1, subsection “结论先行：记忆评测必须贴近真实部署”
findings.issue: The text says the opening module contains two continuous chapters, which is slightly confusing inside an integrated document where this appears as Section 1 followed by Section 2.
findings.recommendation: Rename the reference to “两个连续部分” or explicitly say it previews Section 1 and Section 2.

findings.issue: The grouping is mostly distinct, but experimental findings, engineering implications, and method explanation occasionally overlap across summaries, especially around off-policy/on-policy and external memory trade-offs.
findings.recommendation: Keep section summaries sharper by separating “finding,” “mechanism,” and “engineering implication” into consistent roles.

findings.issue: The artifact follows a clear teaching sequence: motivation, core distinction, framework mechanism, experiments, engineering implications, and final takeaway. Examples, analogies, figures, and caveats support comprehension.
findings.recommendation: No structural revision required; only minor tightening is needed before PDF compilation.

audit.evaluation_context: Teaching-PDF main checkpoint: validate that the integrated Bilibili note has a conclusion-first document structure, coherent chapter sequence, complete teaching progression, aligned titles, and source-faithful synthesis before PDF compilation.
